supporting_sources: 1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv
objective: Define the single-application Rust bounded contexts, their stable published contracts, canonical data ownership, policy/anti-corruption seams, and the capability destination that receives every preserved peer capability — each mapped to exactly one context and contract, with an acyclic context map, single-owner ledger, and explicit migration order.

store_001_alignment.note: The five data-owning contexts (BC-PERSIST, BC-INGEST, BC-BRIDGE, BC-COGNITION, BC-SECRET) reproduce STORE-001's state-class assignments exactly; all other contexts own no durable macro-state and reference BC-PERSIST — preserving the single-durable-writer invariant.

bounded_contexts.name: Canonical Persistence & Semantic Knowledge Graph
bounded_contexts.responsibility: Single canonical durable owner of relational macro-state and semantic/vector/knowledge-graph state; hosts RuVector as a native PostgreSQL extension (No Sidecar).
store_001_state_classes_owned: durable relational macro-state (identities, histories, permissions, provenance, module_path, code AST/rows, causal edges)
semantic / vector / knowledge-graph state (embeddings, hybrid search indexes, GNN causal edges)

bounded_contexts.name: Code Ingestion & High-Frequency Buffer
bounded_contexts.blueprint_layer: Ingestion & Buffering (Nushell parser -> redb buffer; nu_plugin CodeDB)
bounded_contexts.responsibility: Parse the workspace into AST blocks (Nushell) and buffer them at microsecond latency into redb; owns the transient high-frequency tier and the content-addressed BlobStore capture path.
store_001_state_classes_owned: transient high-frequency state (agent thoughts, terminal traces, intermediate logs, BLAKE3 dedup hashes, application-level WAL) [redb canonical]
bounded_contexts.canonical_data_owner: redb (transient tier only); durable blobs are transitional and cut over to BC-PERSIST via verified BlobStore SHA-256 parity

bounded_contexts.name: Forward-Only Bridge, Migration & Materialization
bounded_contexts.blueprint_layer: The Bridge (envctl): redb -> embed -> PostgreSQL; PostgreSQL -> filesystem projection
bounded_contexts.responsibility: Forward-only synchronization of settled redb state into PostgreSQL (embedding via all-MiniLM-L6-v2), replayable migrations, and projection of database-hosted code back to a filesystem directory for musl builds; owns derived projections.
store_001_state_classes_owned: derived projections (materialized source directories for musl compilation, exported release trees)
bounded_contexts.canonical_data_owner: envctl (projection is read-only from PostgreSQL; bridge is the single forward writer path — never a second durable authority)

bounded_contexts.name: Edge Agent Runtime & Cognition
bounded_contexts.responsibility: Host the frozen foundation model in shared memory (ruvllm) and hot-swap per-agent .rvf cognitive containers (MicroLoRA/SONA/FastGRNN); owns per-agent active cognition.
store_001_state_classes_owned: per-agent cognition (MicroLoRA adapters, SONA policy matrices, RL weights) [AgentDB .rvf]
bounded_contexts.canonical_data_owner: AgentDB (.rvf) per agent — non-authoritative for shared macro-state; witnessed by SHAKE256 chains rooted in PostgreSQL source vectors

bounded_contexts.name: Swarm Orchestration, Routing & Temporal Prediction
bounded_contexts.blueprint_layer: Swarm Orchestration & Prediction (Ruflo meta-harness, RuvLTRA, ATAS/ESN)
bounded_contexts.responsibility: Route tasks by Proportional Intelligence (RuvLTRA), run ensemble Temporal-Strange-Attractor forecasts (ATAS/ESN), and coordinate the agent swarm and task runner.
bounded_contexts.canonical_data_owner: none durable — reads codebase/swarm state from BC-PERSIST; transient routing/simulation state is BC-INGEST scratchpad

bounded_contexts.name: Swarm Defense, Policy & Merge Seams
bounded_contexts.blueprint_layer: Defense & Verification (MinCut, COW branching, Cryptographic Witness Chains)
bounded_contexts.responsibility: Enforce policy (PreToolUse denial / agent guard), swarm immune system (MinCut), truth verification (SHAKE256 witness chains / causal-graph), and merge gates (inverse-dependency verification). This is the policy / anti-corruption enforcement context.
bounded_contexts.canonical_data_owner: none durable — monitors the dependency graph and provenance stored in BC-PERSIST; emits typed policy/merge verdicts only

bounded_contexts.name: Governance, Task & Prompt Substrate
bounded_contexts.blueprint_layer: Control-plane task substrate (beads / handoff / prompt registry)
bounded_contexts.responsibility: Agent-first issue/bead tracking, one-way task handoff from the knowledge base, and prompt registry; a subordinate substrate that references PostgreSQL provenance and never becomes a second durable macro-state writer.
bounded_contexts.canonical_data_owner: subordinate stores (beads SQLite/JSONL, prompt registry); references BC-PERSIST provenance; macro-state authority stays in BC-PERSIST

bounded_contexts.name: Source Evidence Ingestion
bounded_contexts.responsibility: Download and extract NotebookLM source material into the evidence corpus that grounds planning claims; an offline producer wrapped behind a process boundary (foreign Python runtime).
bounded_contexts.canonical_data_owner: none durable — writes evidence files consumed downstream by ingestion/verification

bounded_contexts.name: Meta Multi-Repo Control Plane
bounded_contexts.blueprint_layer: gitkb/meta orchestrator (workspace isolation, meta exec, plugin protocol)
bounded_contexts.responsibility: Orchestrate cross-repo operations, run commands across directories (loop engine), and route typed subprocess plugins over MessagePack; isolates machine-generated exhaust from clean Rust during build.

bounded_contexts.name: Toolchain, Build & Portable Release
bounded_contexts.responsibility: Provide the Yazelix workspace toolchain and deterministic static musl compilation of the materialized code into portable install-in-place binaries; coordinate builds.
bounded_contexts.canonical_data_owner: none durable — consumes the materialized projection from BC-BRIDGE to compile release artifacts

bounded_contexts.name: Hardware & GPU Compute Substrate
bounded_contexts.blueprint_layer: Edge inference hardware substrate (GPU / RTX 5090 fleet)
bounded_contexts.responsibility: Expose GPU compute (CUDA driver-API bindings) behind a safe compute trait and maintain hardware inventory facts for edge-inference placement.
bounded_contexts.canonical_data_owner: none durable — hardware/compute substrate; inventory facts only

bounded_contexts.name: Home & Network Domain
bounded_contexts.responsibility: HomeLab network control for the LifeOS `home` workspace; durable home/network state is persisted to the canonical store, not held in a local durable authority.
bounded_contexts.canonical_data_owner: none durable — home/network state persists to BC-PERSIST (single durable writer)

bounded_contexts.name: Developer Experience & Read Tooling
bounded_contexts.responsibility: Read-only developer surfaces: bead viewer, Claude Code board/session TUI, and a token-minimizing CLI proxy; consume upstream contracts without owning durable state.
bounded_contexts.canonical_data_owner: none durable — read-only projections over BC-GOVERNANCE / session state

bounded_contexts.name: Secret & Provenance Authority
bounded_contexts.blueprint_layer: External secret authority (OS keyring + secretd + vault)
bounded_contexts.responsibility: Hold API keys, attestation bundles, and source pins in the OS keyring / external vault; LifeOS core holds references only. redb never holds secrets; PostgreSQL stores provenance references, never raw secrets.
store_001_state_classes_owned: external-secret / provenance authority (API keys, attestation bundles, source pins)

bounded_contexts.name: LifeOS Presentation & UDS Shell (consolidation host)
bounded_contexts.blueprint_layer: LifeOS UI / Unix Domain Sockets (the Vue3+Tauri host receiving all peers)
bounded_contexts.responsibility: The single-application host: Vue3+Vite+Pinia+Tauri2 presentation shell that renders swarm/agent status by reading the same memory-mapped redb state and PostgreSQL via UDS/IPC (read-only sink). This is the consolidation target that receives every peer capability.
bounded_contexts.canonical_data_owner: none durable — read-only UDS/IPC client over BC-INGEST (redb), BC-PERSIST, BC-ORCHESTRATION, BC-GOVERNANCE, BC-COGNITION

context_map.edge_semantics: edge from->to means 'from' depends on / consumes the published contract of 'to' (customer -> supplier).
context_map.cycle_check_method: Kahn topological sort; acyclic iff every node is emitted.

capability_assignments.capability_boundary: Nushell CodeDB plugin: redb + PgStore blob stores, BlobStore parity, fail-closed, MCP.
capability_assignments.data_ownership: Owns the redb transient high-frequency buffer (STORE-001 class: transient, redb-canonical) and the CodeDB parser/capture path; its durable source-blob store is TRANSITIONAL and cuts over redb->PostgreSQL via the BlobStore SHA-256 content-addressed parity contract, one verified swap at a time. No second durable writer.
capability_assignments.store_001_relationship: Owns the redb transient high-frequency buffer (STORE-001 class: transient, redb-canonical) and the CodeDB parser/capture path; its durable source-blob store is TRANSITIONAL and cuts over redb->PostgreSQL via the BlobStore SHA-256 content-addressed parity contract, one verified swap at a time. No second durable writer.
capability_assignments.dependency_edges: -> lifeos ADOPT-001 / STORE-001 (redb & PostgreSQL CodeDB); POSTGRES-004
capability_assignments.parity_gate: cargo test --all  =>  cargo test: `test result: ok` (0 failed) on all crates
capability_assignments.no_loss_parity_gate: adopt-in-place at pinned HEAD; parity = `cargo test --all` green before AND after any move; upgrade-only (North Star) — no capability removed, no downgrade.
capability_assignments.rollback: Restore peer to pinned HEAD at its recorded source path; re-run parity command; adopt-in-place is reversible (no source mutated during planning).
capability_assignments.proof_task: CONSOLIDATE-001 (this) -> per-context architecture-decision task (next_action); adoption executed under LIFEOS-004 with peer parity green before AND after.

capability_assignments.capability_boundary: Neuromorphic vector search engine (spiking NN + HNSW) — RuVector core.
capability_assignments.data_ownership: RuVector native PostgreSQL extension: canonical owner of semantic/vector/knowledge-graph state (STORE-001 class: semantic). No Sidecar; full cutover waits on live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation per STORE-001 open dependency.
capability_assignments.store_001_relationship: RuVector native PostgreSQL extension: canonical owner of semantic/vector/knowledge-graph state (STORE-001 class: semantic). No Sidecar; full cutover waits on live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation per STORE-001 open dependency.
capability_assignments.dependency_edges: -> lifeos POSTGRES-005 RuVector semantics; PGAUTH cognition

capability_assignments.capability_boundary: Env/secret control plane: secretd/secretctl, migration engine, libSQL/redb store.
capability_assignments.data_ownership: Forward-only bridge writer (redb->embed->PostgreSQL) and projection engine (PostgreSQL->filesystem); owns derived projections (STORE-001 class: derived projections). Its secret sub-plane surfaces to BC-SECRET; its remote libSQL store is subordinate to PostgreSQL (deferred to STORE-001). Never a second durable authority.
capability_assignments.store_001_relationship: Forward-only bridge writer (redb->embed->PostgreSQL) and projection engine (PostgreSQL->filesystem); owns derived projections (STORE-001 class: derived projections). Its secret sub-plane surfaces to BC-SECRET; its remote libSQL store is subordinate to PostgreSQL (deferred to STORE-001). Never a second durable authority.

anti_corruption_seams.wrap_mechanism: BlobStore trait with SHA-256 content-addressed identity; redb-durable is TRANSITIONAL and migrates to PostgreSQL only via a green live blobstore_parity.rs run — never by assumption (STORE-001 verified-cutover invariant).
anti_corruption_seams.grounded_source: STORE-001 state_class_assignments[0].migration_path + invariants[Verified cutover]; ADOPT-001 BlobStore trait

anti_corruption_seams.wrap_mechanism: RuVector runs as a NATIVE PostgreSQL extension (No Sidecar); f32 distance math never leaks a second vector database. Full cutover gated on live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation.
anti_corruption_seams.grounded_source: Blueprint 'No Sidecar' / Trojan Horse; STORE-001 invariant[No sidecar] + open_dependencies[0]

anti_corruption_seams.wrap_mechanism: Subprocess/IPC adapter translating node workflow events into the Rust SwarmRouter contract; node types never enter the core.
anti_corruption_seams.grounded_source: PRESERVE-002 peer ruflo (node); Blueprint Ruflo meta-harness

anti_corruption_seams.wrap_mechanism: Message-bus adapter translating hermes messages into the AgentRuntime contract at a process boundary; foreign runtime types never enter the core.

anti_corruption_seams.foreign_model: Claude Code PreToolUse hook payloads (agent guard)
anti_corruption_seams.wrap_mechanism: Policy adapter that converts external hook payloads into typed policy verdicts (allow/deny); external hook schema never enters the domain model. This is the policy seam.
anti_corruption_seams.grounded_source: PRESERVE-002 peer agent (Claude Code agent guard); Blueprint Defense & Verification

anti_corruption_seams.wrap_mechanism: Unsafe FFI isolated behind a safe GpuComputeSeam trait; raw CUDA driver types never escape the seam.
anti_corruption_seams.grounded_source: PRESERVE-002 peer cuda-oxide; Blueprint Edge Inference hardware

anti_corruption_seams.wrap_mechanism: License + process boundary: the MPL-2.0 editor runs as an external process and is NOT statically linked into the LifeOS core binary, keeping license obligations isolated.
Secret authority stays external (OS keyring / vault); LifeOS core holds references only, never raw secrets. redb never holds secrets; PostgreSQL stores provenance references only.
Wrapped behind CanonicalStore as SUBORDINATE to PostgreSQL; never a second durable writer; final store role deferred to STORE-001.
anti_corruption_seams.grounded_source: adopted_capabilities.source.csv 'Remote libSQL store' (ADOPT-002, deferred-to-STORE-001); STORE-001 single-durable-writer invariant

anti_corruption_seams.wrap_mechanism: Typed MessagePack plugin protocol at a subprocess boundary; gitkb/meta isolates napi-rs/wasm exhaust from clean Rust core.
anti_corruption_seams.grounded_source: Blueprint gitkb/meta 'Dirty Repo' isolation + MessagePack protocol; PRESERVE-002 meta-plugins/meta_plugin_protocol

anti_corruption_seams.wrap_mechanism: Repository adapter: beads is a SUBORDINATE substrate referencing PostgreSQL provenance; it is not a second durable macro-state writer.
anti_corruption_seams.grounded_source: PRESERVE-002 peer beads_rust; STORE-001 single-durable-writer invariant

anti_corruption_seams.wrap_mechanism: Offline process-boundary CLI wrapper; JSON/file handoff; no Python types enter the Rust core.

migration_order.rationale: Land the STORE-001 data spine first: PostgreSQL+RuVector canonical owner, redb transient buffer, envctl forward bridge/projection, and the external secret authority the bridge depends on. Any redb-durable class moves to PostgreSQL ONLY via a green live blobstore_parity.rs run (verified-cutover invariant).
migration_order.gate: STORE-001 invariants hold: single durable writer, forward-only sync, verified cutover, no sidecar, provenance preserved.

migration_order.rationale: Bring up the compute substrate (GPU seam + hardware inventory) then the edge agent runtime (ruvllm console + AgentDB .rvf), which depend only on BC-PERSIST and BC-COMPUTE.
migration_order.gate: AgentDB cognition non-authoritative; witnessed by SHAKE256 chains rooted in PostgreSQL; GPU FFI isolated behind the compute trait.

migration_order.rationale: Integrity/policy seams before the swarm they govern (ORCHESTRATION depends-on INTEGRITY); governance substrate alongside. All depend only on already-landed roots.
migration_order.gate: Policy verdicts typed; MinCut/witness-chain enforced; governance substrate subordinate to BC-PERSIST provenance.

migration_order.rationale: Foundation build (consumes BC-BRIDGE projection) then the control plane (consumes BC-FOUNDATION); the offline evidence pipeline lands here as an isolated producer.
migration_order.gate: Static musl portable build from materialized projection; plugin/protocol + evidence at process boundaries.

migration_order.rationale: Wire the presentation sink (reads everything via UDS/IPC), read-only devex surfaces, and the home/network domain last — pure consumers with no upstream dependents.
migration_order.gate: Read-only UDS/IPC; no durable ownership introduced; home/network state persists to BC-PERSIST.

migration_order.rationale: Reconcile the 9 transient worktrees into their parent capabilities per PRESERVE-003 (merge upstream or archive). They are NOT adopted as independent capabilities and NEVER become second owners.
migration_order.gate: PRESERVE-003 topology-transition gate: exact source/target, pre/post checksums, git-ref equivalence, worktree repair, rollback.
